upload.title: Upload Document
upload.subtitle: Adding knowledge to the RAG system

sparseEmbedding.label: Generate Sparse Embeddings (For Hybrid Search)
sparseEmbedding.warning: Warning: This will increase processing time. If unchecked, retrieval scores might be lower if the Knowledge Base is configured for Hybrid Search.

sentence.title: Sentence Splitter
sentence.desc: Splits text by sentence boundaries (periods, question marks, etc.). Keeps sentences intact.
sentence.usage: General RAG applications, articles, and prose where sentence context matters.

token.title: Token Splitter
token.desc: Splits text based on raw token count. This is a strict, mechanical split.
token.usage: When you need to strictly fit content into specific LLM context windows (e.g., exactly 512 tokens).

character.title: Character Splitter
character.desc: Splits text based on a fixed number of characters and a separator.
character.usage: Precise format control or when tokenizers are unavailable.

word.title: Word Splitter
word.usage: Basic text processing where semantic boundaries are less critical.

recursive.title: Recursive Character Splitter
recursive.desc: Recursively tries different separators (paragraphs, newlines, spaces) to find the best split point.
recursive.usage: Code, technical documents, and markdown where structure (paragraphs, functions) should be preserved.

sliding.title: Sliding Window
sliding.desc: Creates overlapping chunks by sliding a fixed-size window across the text.
sliding.usage: Time-series data or when ensuring absolutely zero context loss at boundaries is critical.

semantic.title: Semantic Splitter
semantic.desc: Uses embedding similarity to group meaningfully related text together, rather than arbitrary size limits.
semantic.usage: High-quality RAG where topics vary significantly within a document.

hierarchical.title: Hierarchical Splitter
hierarchical.desc: Creates chunks at multiple levels of granularity (e.g., Parent chunk -> Child chunks).
hierarchical.usage: Advanced retrieval scenarios (get small chunk for match, return large chunk for context).

validation.invalidChunkSize: Chunk size must be greater than 0
validation.overlapTooLarge: Chunk overlap must be less than chunk size
validation.invalidWindowSize: Window size must be at least 1
validation.invalidBufferSize: Buffer size must be at least 1
validation.invalidThreshold: Threshold percentage must be between 0 and 100
validation.invalidHierarchical: Chunk sizes must be a valid JSON list of positive integers
validation.hierarchicalOrder: Sizes must be in descending order (e.g., [1024, 512, 128])

preview.title: Chunking Result Preview
preview.subtitle: Test how the system breaks down your document
preview.samplePlaceholder: Enter a long piece of text to test...
preview.defaultExample:
Deep learning is a subset of machine learning, which is essentially a neural network with three or more layers. These neural networks attempt to simulate the behavior of the human brain—albeit far from matching its ability—allowing it to “learn” from large amounts of data. While a neural network with a single layer can still make approximate predictions, additional hidden layers can help to optimize and refine for accuracy.

Deep learning drives many artificial intelligence (AI) applications and services that improve automation, performing analytical and physical tasks without human intervention. Deep learning technology lies behind everyday products and services (such as digital assistants, voice-enabled TV remotes, and credit card fraud detection) as well as emerging technologies (such as self-driving cars).

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique for enhancing the accuracy and reliability of generative AI models with facts fetched from external sources. By connecting LLMs to private data, RAG models can deliver more contextually relevant, accurate, and up-to-date information.

### Key Benefits of RAG:
1. **Accuracy**: Reduces hallucinations by grounding responses on verifiable facts.
2. **Freshness**: Accesses real-time data without the need for expensive model retraining.
3. **Security**: Keeps sensitive enterprise data within your private infrastructure.
